betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Redstone Relay§f

§aTransmit redstone signals through duct networks for remote automation and control!§f

§bRedstone Relays§f are attachments installed on §bFluxduct§f, §bFluiduct§f, §bItemduct§f, or §bStructuralduct§f connections. They carry redstone signals through the duct network alongside whatever the duct is transporting.

§6Crafting§f:
§e1× Redstone§f + §e1× Nether Quartz§f + §e2× Signalum Nugget§f + §e2× Lead Ingot§f → §e1× Redstone Relay§f.

§6Transfer Modes§f:
§b• Redstone Input§f — Reads signal from adjacent block, transmits through ducts (default).
§b• Redstone Output§f — Receives signal from ducts, outputs to adjacent block.
§b• Comparator Input§f — Measures adjacent block like a comparator, transmits strength.

§6Signal Modes§f:
Scaled, Inverted Scaled, Threshold, Inverted Threshold.

§6Channels§f:
§e16 color-coded channels§f. Relays only communicate on the same channel — run §e16 independent signals§f through one duct network.

§eTip§f: Use distinct color channels for separate automation lines. A single itemduct network can simultaneously route items, fluids, and redstone control signals without interference.
